commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Christ had said: “Not what goes into the mouth defiles a man but what comes out of the mouth—this defiles a man.” This applies to food as well. .

commentaries.author: Diodorus of Tarsus
commentaries.content: This means that nothing is common or unclean when eaten with faith in Christ. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Nothing is unclean of itself. Because the law that made them unclean, is not now binding of itself, yet a man must not act against his conscience, neither must he, when he can avoid it, scandalize or offend the weak: nor cause divisions or dissensions. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Nothing is unclean by nature, but it becomes so by the spirit in which a person uses it.
